checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end 2010-12-31 value 74361000; end 2011-12-31 value 87736000; end 2012-12-31 value 88958000

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: GrossProfit FY2008 211074000 / SalesRevenueGoodsNet FY2008 250082000; GrossProfit FY2012 466846000 / SalesRevenueGoodsNet FY2012 620255000
evidence.location: Income statement, GrossProfit and Revenue FY2008 & FY2012

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.location: Balance sheet notes, allowance for doubtful accounts 2009 & 2012

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: UPLOAD filing_date 2010-07-30; CORRESP filing_date 2010-08-11; CT ORDER filing_date 2010-08-20; UPLOAD filing_date 2010-08-30; CORRESP filing_date 2010-09-03; UPLOAD filing_date 2010-09-28; CORRESP filing_date 2010-10-12
evidence.location: Filing chronology list, 2010 SEC correspondence cluster

evidence.quote: UPLOAD filing_date 2012-09-14; CORRESP filing_date 2012-09-27; UPLOAD filing_date 2012-10-18
evidence.location: Filing chronology list, 2012 SEC correspondence cluster

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data spanning FY2008 through Q1 2013 across Assets, Revenue, NetIncomeLoss, Cash Flow, Inventory, Receivables, Goodwill, and reserve accounts.

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Understatement of bad-debt allowance relative to growing receivables and failure to grow accrued liabilities in line with revenue could understate expense recognition (bad debt expense, accrued operating expenses), thereby overstating reported net income in the periods where reserves declined.

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent end 2009-12-31 4163000; end 2012-12-31 2780000 vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end 2009-12-31 58462000; end 2012-12-31 88958000
AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent end 2010-12-31 25989000; end 2012-12-31 20880000 vs SalesRevenueGoodsNet FY2010 478237000; FY2012 620255000
rationale_evidence.location: Balance sheet, accrued liabilities 2010 & 2012; Income statement revenue 2010 & 2012

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Rapid growth in goodwill (post-acquisition) and other non-current assets far outpacing revenue growth could reflect aggressive capitalization of costs (or optimistic purchase-price allocations) that defer expense recognition, inflating reported assets and understating current period expenses.

rationale_evidence.location: Balance sheet Goodwill 2010-2011; Income statement Revenue FY2011

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Large and growing divergence between reported net income and operating cash flow (e.g., FY2011 net loss vs. positive OCF; FY2012 near-breakeven net income vs. much larger OCF) suggests significant non-cash charges/accruals (potentially litigation-related or other one-time reserves) whose timing does not match cash realization, creating a mechanism for earnings-cash flow timing shifts that merits further scrutiny of accrual assumptions.

rationale_evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss 2011-01-01 to 2011-12-31 value -69849000 vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities 2011-01-01 to 2011-12-31 value 62965000
rationale_evidence.location: FY2011 Income Statement and Cash Flow Statement

rationale_evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss 2012-01-01 to 2012-12-31 value 3144000 vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities 2012-01-01 to 2012-12-31 value 130082000
rationale_evidence.location: FY2012 Income Statement and Cash Flow Statement

top_signals: Persistent and large divergence between net income and operating cash flow in FY2011 (-$69.8M NI vs +$63.0M OCF) and FY2012 ($3.1M NI vs $130.1M OCF)

Declining allowance-for-doubtful-accounts ratio (7.1% of AR in 2009 to 3.1% in 2012) and declining accrued liabilities ($26.0M in 2010 to $20.9M in 2012) despite ~30-52% growth in receivables/revenue over the same span
Non-current 'soft' assets (Goodwill +54.6% in 2011, Other Assets Noncurrent +90.8% in 2011 and +37.5% in 2012) growing much faster than revenue (13.0%-14.8% annually)

10-K/A amendment filed three months after the original FY2009 10-K, plus two extended clusters of SEC UPLOAD/CORRESP/CT ORDER correspondence in mid-2010 and late-2012 suggesting active regulatory review
